labels.alkalinityReductionFromCaMgPpmCaCO3: Alkalinity reduction from Ca/Mg (ppm as CaCO3)

notes.counterIonsOnlyStrongAcids: Counter-ions: only sulfuric adds SO4 and only hydrochloric adds Cl. For other acids these are 0.

salt_additions.v1:
ppm = mg/L

For each salt:
- Convert grams -> moles of salt
- Use stoichiometry to get moles of each ion group
- Convert to mg of each ion group
- Divide by volume (L) to get ppm

Final ions = base ions + sum(per-salt deltas)

acidification.v1:
We solve for an acid amount that reaches a target pH given alkalinity and volume.

Key relationships (simplified):
- final_alkalinity = effective_alkalinity - acid_required(mEq/L) × 50
- acid_required depends on carbonate distribution + acid dissociation at target pH

Counter-ions:
- sulfuric adds SO4
- hydrochloric adds Cl

mash_overall.v1:
Overall ions = ions(after salts) + acid counter-ions, and HCO3 is derived from final alkalinity.

This combines:
- salt additions (mass-balance on ppm)
- acidification (final alkalinity + SO4/Cl counter-ions)

sparge_overall.v1: Overall ions = ions(after salts) + acid counter-ions, and HCO3 is derived from final alkalinity.

boil_overall.v1: Overall ions = ions(after salts) + acid counter-ions, and HCO3 is derived from final alkalinity.

pre_boil_volume.v1: pre_boil_volume = mash_water + sparge_water - grain_absorption - mash_losses - leftover + boil_additions

og.v1: OG is estimated from fermentables yield, efficiency (%), and kettle volume.

pbg.v1: PBG is estimated from fermentables yield, efficiency (%), and pre-boil volume.

attenuation.v1: Effective attenuation = average of the top 2 yeast attenuations (overrides win).

ibu_tinseth.v1: Tinseth IBU is computed from hop additions, utilization, boil gravity, and post-boil volume.

ibu_rager.v1: Rager IBU is computed from hop additions, utilization, boil gravity, and post-boil volume.

mcu.v1: MCU (Malt Color Units) = Σ(lb × °L) / gal, using post-boil/kettle volume.

common.toggleHint: Applies to all sections on this page (where available).
common.excludeDryhop: dry hop does not contribute to IBU
common.noteDependsOnWaterAndEquipment: Depends on saved water settings + equipment snapshot.
common.noteMissingWaterSettings: Missing saved water settings; volume estimates may be unavailable.
common.noteMissingFermentableColors: Missing fermentable color (Lovibond) on one or more grist rows.

abv.title: ABV (estimated)
abv.body:
Formula:
ABV = (OG - FG) × 131.25

This recipe:
OG = {og}
FG = {fg}
ABV = {abv}%

Notes:
- OG/FG are derived estimates (not measured).

ibuTinseth.title: IBU (Tinseth, estimated)
ibuTinseth.body:
Model (Tinseth):
U = 1.65 × 0.000125^(G - 1) × (1 - e^(-0.04 × t)) / 4.15
IBU = Σ (g × (AA%/100) × U × 1000 / V_L)

This recipe:
Gravity used (SG) = {gravity} ({gravitySource})
Volume used (L) = {volume} ({volumeSource})

Hop additions (from editor):
{hopsLines}

Result:
IBU (Tinseth) = {ibu}

ibuRager.title: IBU (Rager, estimated)
ibuRager.body:
Model (Rager):
U% ≈ 18.11 + 13.86 × tanh((t - 31.32) / 18.27)
Gravity adjustment (if G > 1.050): GA = (G - 1.050) / 0.2
IBU = Σ (g × (AA%/100) × (U%/(1+GA)) × 1000 / V_L)

This recipe:
Gravity used (SG) = {gravity} ({gravitySource})
Volume used (L) = {volume} ({volumeSource})

Hop additions (from editor):
{hopsLines}

Result:
IBU (Rager) = {ibu}

srmMorey.title: Color (SRM, Morey, estimated)
srmMorey.body:
Model (Morey):
MCU = Σ (lb × °L) / gal
SRM = 1.4922 × MCU^0.6859

This recipe:
Kettle volume used = {volume} L
Notes:
- {notes}

Result:
SRM (Morey) = {srm}

srmDaniels.title: Color (SRM, Daniels, estimated)
srmDaniels.body:
Model (Daniels, linear):
MCU = Σ (lb × °L) / gal
SRM ≈ 0.2 × MCU + 8.4

This recipe:
Kettle volume used = {volume} L
Notes:
- {notes}

Result:
SRM (Daniels) = {srm}

kettleVolume.title: Volume (kettle)
kettleVolume.body:
This value is derived from your saved water settings and equipment losses/evaporation.

This recipe:
Kettle volume = {kettleVolume} L

Notes:
- {notes}

preBoilVolume.title: Pre-boil volume (estimated)
preBoilVolume.body:
This value is derived from your saved water settings and equipment losses.

This recipe:
Pre-boil volume = {preBoilVolume} L

Notes:
- {notes}

og.title: OG (estimated)
og.body:
OG is estimated from fermentables potential + volume + efficiency.

This recipe:
OG = {og}
Volume (kettle) = {volume} L
Efficiency used = {efficiency}%

Notes:
- If efficiency/fermentables are missing, OG can be unavailable.

fg.title: FG (estimated)

attenuation.title: Attenuation (effective)
attenuation.body:
Model:
- For each yeast: use override% if present, else use BeerJSON attenuation (avg(min,max) if given).
- Effective attenuation = average of the top 2 highest effective values.

This recipe:
All yeasts:
{yeastLines}

Selected (top 2):
{selectedLines}

Top-2 average = {topAvg}%
Effective attenuation = {attenuation}%

pbg.title: PBG (pre-boil gravity, estimated)
pbg.body:
PBG is estimated like OG, but using the pre-boil volume.

This recipe:
PBG = {pbg}
Pre-boil volume = {preBoilVolume} L
Efficiency used = {efficiency}%

Notes:
- Requires saved water volumes to estimate pre-boil volume.

acidRequired.title: Acid required (target pH)
acidRequired.body:
Conceptual model:
- We solve for an acid amount that brings Starting pH -> Target pH, given Starting alkalinity and water volume.

Notes:
- If the selected profile has no pH, we only use the manually entered Starting pH.
- Acid type/strength changes the conversion from equivalents to mL/grams.
- This is a heuristic calculator: use it as guidance, then measure/adjust.

finalAlkalinity.title: Final alkalinity (ppm as CaCO3)
finalAlkalinity.body:
Meaning:
- Alkalinity after the calculated acid addition.

Notes:
- It can be near 0 when acid neutralizes the buffering capacity.
- We report alkalinity in ppm as CaCO3 (standard brewing convention).
finalAlkalinity.bodyWithValues:
Meaning:
- Alkalinity after the calculated acid addition.

This recipe:
Starting alkalinity = {startingAlk} ppm as CaCO3
Final alkalinity = {finalAlk} ppm as CaCO3

Notes:
- It can be near 0 when acid neutralizes the buffering capacity.
- We report alkalinity in ppm as CaCO3 (standard brewing convention).

ionsAfterSalts.title: Resulting ions (after salts only)
ionsAfterSalts.body:
Meaning:
- Starting from the selected sparge profile, we add ions contributed by the entered salt grams at the given sparge water volume.

Notes:
- Values are ppm (mg/L) for each ion.
ionsAfterSalts.bodyWithValues:
Meaning:
- Starting from the selected sparge profile, we add ions contributed by the entered salt grams at the given sparge water volume.

This recipe:
Volume = {volumeL} L

Salts:
{saltsLines}

Ions (ppm):
{ionsLines}

Notes:
- Values are ppm (mg/L) for each ion.

ionsAfterSaltsAndAcid.title: Resulting ions (after salts + acid)
ionsAfterSaltsAndAcid.body:
Meaning:
- Same as “after salts”, then apply the acid result.

Notes:
- SO4/Cl increases come from the acid counter-ion model (when applicable).
- HCO3 is derived from alkalinity (proxy), not measured bicarbonate.
ionsAfterSaltsAndAcid.bodyWithValues:
Meaning:
- Same as “after salts”, then apply the acid result.

This recipe:
Volume = {volumeL} L
Final alkalinity = {finalAlk} ppm as CaCO3

Salts:
{saltsLines}

Ions (ppm):
{ionsLines}

Notes:
- SO4/Cl increases come from the acid counter-ion model (when applicable).
- HCO3 is derived from alkalinity (proxy), not measured bicarbonate.

alkalinityHeuristic.title: Heuristic: Ca/Mg reduce effective alkalinity
alkalinityHeuristic.body:
We apply a residual-alkalinity-style adjustment:
- Calcium and magnesium from salts modestly reduce effective alkalinity, so acid required can change slightly when salts are added.

Notes:
- This is intentionally modest; it improves realism without overfitting.

acidRequired.title: Acid required (target pH)
acidRequired.body:
Conceptual model:
- We solve for an acid amount that brings Starting pH -> Target pH, given Starting alkalinity and mash water volume.

Notes:
- Acid type/strength changes the conversion from equivalents to mL/grams.
- This is a heuristic calculator: use it as guidance, then measure/adjust.
acidRequired.bodyWithValues:
Conceptual model:
- We solve for an acid amount that brings Starting pH -> Target pH, given Starting alkalinity and mash water volume.

This recipe:
Starting pH = {startingPh}
Target pH = {targetPh}
Starting alkalinity = {startingAlk} ppm as CaCO3
Mash water volume = {volumeL} L

Result:
{detailsLines}

Notes:
- Acid type/strength changes the conversion from equivalents to mL/grams.
- This is a heuristic calculator: use it as guidance, then measure/adjust.

- We report alkalinity in ppm as CaCO3 (standard brewing convention).

ionsAfterSalts.title: Resulting ions (after salts only)
ionsAfterSalts.body:
Meaning:
- Starting from the mixed source water, we add ions contributed by the entered salt grams.

Notes:
- Values are ppm (mg/L) for each ion.
- This table does not include acid; see the overall result for combined output.
ionsAfterSalts.bodyWithValues:
Meaning:
- Starting from the mixed source water, we add ions contributed by the entered salt grams.

This recipe:

Salts:
{saltsLines}

Ions (ppm):
{ionsLines}

Notes:
- Values are ppm (mg/L) for each ion.
- This table does not include acid; see the overall result for combined output.

overallSnapshot.title: Overall mash snapshot (salts + acid)
overallSnapshot.body:
Meaning:
- Combined result after applying both salts and acid.

Notes:
- SO4/Cl increases come from the acid counter-ion model (when applicable).
- HCO3 is derived from alkalinity (proxy), not measured bicarbonate.
overallSnapshot.bodyWithValues:
Meaning:
- Combined result after applying both salts and acid.

This recipe:
pH = {ph}
Mash water volume = {volumeL} L
Final alkalinity = {finalAlk} ppm as CaCO3

Ions (ppm):
{ionsLines}

Notes:
- SO4/Cl increases come from the acid counter-ion model (when applicable).
- HCO3 is derived from alkalinity (proxy), not measured bicarbonate.

mergedWaterRecap.title: Merged water recap
mergedWaterRecap.body:
Meaning:
- This section summarizes each stream (mash/sparge/additional boil) and the merged summary.

Notes:
- Merged pH is an approximate volume-weighted [H+] mix (rule-of-thumb).
- Streams without saved snapshots are excluded from merged ions.
mergedWaterRecap.bodyWithValues:
Meaning:
- This section summarizes each stream (mash/sparge/additional boil) and the merged summary.

This recipe:
Total volume = {totalVolumeL} L
Approx. merged pH = {mergedPh}
Merged final alkalinity = {mergedFinalAlk} ppm as CaCO3

Streams:
{streamLines}

Notes:
- Merged pH is an approximate volume-weighted [H+] mix (rule-of-thumb).
- Streams without saved snapshots are excluded from merged ions.
mergedWaterRecap.streamLine: - {label}: {volumeL} L, pH {ph}, alk {alk} ppm as CaCO3

mergedIons.title: Merged ions (ppm)
mergedIons.body:
Meaning:
- Computed from saved mash/sparge/boil snapshots after salts + acid (SO4/Cl counter-ions only) and averaged by volume.

Notes:
- Streams without saved snapshots are excluded.
- HCO3 is derived from alkalinity (proxy), not measured bicarbonate.
mergedIons.bodyWithValues:
Meaning:
- Computed from saved mash/sparge/boil snapshots after salts + acid (SO4/Cl counter-ions only) and averaged by volume.

This recipe:
Ions (ppm):
{ionsLines}

Notes:
- Streams without saved snapshots are excluded.
- HCO3 is derived from alkalinity (proxy), not measured bicarbonate.
